tickets.question: Does make_installer.sh produce a Developer-ID-signed + notarised installer, with Apple's notary service returning success (stapled ticket)?
tickets.notes: Run make_installer.sh (Developer ID sign + THETA_NOTARY notarisation). If this fails it is the first ticket — everything downstream depends on it.

tickets.question: Does the notarised installer launch without Gatekeeper/quarantine blocking (no 'unidentified developer', 'damaged', or quarantine warning)?
tickets.notes: Install via the notarised installer on the clean Mac, then launch the host. Record the EXACT dialog wording if anything appears. Expected on a working notarised build: no block.

tickets.question: Does the plain ad-hoc (codesign --sign -) build get blocked on the same clean rig, confirming notarisation is the fix?
tickets.notes: Optional A/B: side-load the ad-hoc build on the clean rig and confirm it DOES get blocked.

tickets.question: Is CATHODE.vst3 present in /Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/VST3 and seen by the host?
Is CATHODE.component in /Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/Components and does it pass the host's first-launch AU scan?
tickets.notes: Clean rig is safe to let it scan; nothing to re-authorise.

tickets.question: Does CATHODE load and instantiate on a track in Ableton Live, with the window opening?
Does CATHODE pass AU validation in Logic and instantiate?
Does CATHODE load in one more host (Reaper / Bitwig / Studio One) to confirm it is not host-specific?
Does the window open at 1280x640, render fully, and show 'v16.96'?
Does adding/removing the plugin and opening/closing the UI ~10x stay stable (no crash, no creeping slowdown)?
Does a saved project with CATHODE + custom settings reopen with state intact?
Do host bypass and plugin BYPASS both null cleanly with no level jump?
On drums, does ATTACK punch the onset above noon and soften it below noon, with the meter spiking to match?
Does SUSTAIN shape the body just after the hit in the correct direction, audibly?
Does DECAY (new) shape the tail (up = longer/fuller, down = tighter) and read clearly distinct from SUSTAIN?
tickets.notes: New transient control in this build.

tickets.question: With heavy CRUSH/WARBLE running, does flipping LFO INPUT to CHAIN END move where the shaping lands, with no click/pop on the switch?
With TONE depth armed on a full-band source, is the dark-to-bright wobble clearly stronger (now +/-15 dB) yet still usable at full depth, not blown out?
Do VOL / PAN / WIDTH / TONE / WARBLE each do nothing at depth 0 and engage as depth comes up?
Do all 8 factory LFO presets load and sound sane, with transient params neutral and routing = LFO input on load?
Does AUTO LEVEL on/off change the output-ceiling behaviour with no instability?
Do LT_TRANS_DEC, LT_TRANS_POS and a few other params automate cleanly (track, no zipper noise, no crash)?
At 44.1 / 48 / 96 kHz, do the transient meter and DSP behave with no broken-sounding tuning?
At 64 and 1024 sample buffers, is there no glitch or crackle?
Is silence-in silence-out (no idle hiss), and does hammering params during playback produce no NaN blow-up?
Over a few minutes with LFO + transient both active, is there no drift and no crash?
Does the button read TRANS (not EDIT), click into the transient page, and BACK return to main?
Does the live gain meter scroll with 'now' at the right edge, lift on boost and drop on cut (+/-12 dB scale), and show live gain in dB + routing text in the title row?
Are the six main rows in order (SHAPE/SYNC, VOL/PAN, WIDTH/TONE, WARBLE/RATE, PHASE/SMOOTH, OUTPUT WIDTH full-width) with no overlap or clipping at the 1280 minimum width?
Is the DECAY pill present, labelled, and sitting cleanly beside the routing toggle?
Does the routing toggle read LFO INPUT / CHAIN END, flip on click, and follow a preset that sets it?
Is LOSS now olive (matching DULL and the other sliders), with no other stray gold or odd-coloured pills anywhere?
tickets.notes: Sweep the whole UI for stray colours.

tickets.question: Does the LFO scope still show on both pages and track the shape?
Bouncing main to TRANS to scope repeatedly, does nothing get stuck shown/hidden and the meter resume cleanly?
Does every slider/knob/dropdown on every module move and read correctly, with correct labels?
Does 'v16.96' appear everywhere the version is shown?
Do the logo and THETA / AUDIO RESEARCH render correctly?
With ATTACK/SUSTAIN now reading off the fast/mid envelope pair (changed in v16.95), does the onset response feel right, or does it want re-tuning before beta?
tickets.notes: Flag now if it wants re-tuning.

tickets.question: Any visual glitch, mislabel, or rough edge you would not want a tester to see?
Confirm this build is VST3 + AU only. AAX is the next build; do not test Pro Tools here.
tickets.notes: Scope note, not a Pro Tools test.
